title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
As the group returns to the mainland, Officer Vane informs Andrew G. Tewell that he will spend the night with the state police. District Attorney Escham expresses skepticism regarding Stephen Megalos's explanation that the Tewell brothers fled their homeland and changed their names due to a childhood motive. Ellery Queen counters this, suggesting the brothers were actually fleeing a more direct danger, and advises a transatlantic investigation, specifically recommending a telegram be sent to Yugoslavia.

While walking around the east side of the house with Professor Yadley and Escham, the group is approached by Dr. Temple and Jonah Lincoln. Jonah appears agitated by the recent strange events at Bredehede and expresses frustration regarding his sister Hester's recent return from Oyster Island. A sudden, piercing cackle from the house draws Dr. Temple and the others to the second floor, where they discover Hester in a state of hysterical madness. As Dr. Temple and Officer Vane attempt to calm her, Hester directs intense, hateful verbal attacks toward Mrs. Bradley before collapsing.

After Dr. Temple clears the room to administer a sedative, Jonah explains that Hester had been infatuated with a man named Roman on Oyster Island but has decided to move to New York to be alone. When Elijah asks why Hester is so opposed to Mrs. Bradley, Jonah's demeanor shifts, and he firmly dismisses the question, claiming he has no knowledge of the motive.
